DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS AMBER INDIA CORPORATION, AMBER INDIA COMMERCIAL, INC, VIJAY BIST, AMBER INDIA ENTERPRISE, INC and VIJAY KUMAR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained in part, and denied in part. Defendants' demurrer to the entire complaint on the basis that Plaintiffs have failed to plead facts to support their class action claims is denied. Whenever there is a reasonable possibility that the plaintiffs can plead a prima facie community of interest among class members, the preferred course is to defer decision on the propriety of the class action until an evidentiary hearing has been held on the appropriateness of class litigation. (See Canon U.S.A., Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 1.) Plaintiffs have met their initial burden by pleading that Defendants have adopted a policy of denying bussers and servers meal and rest breaks, failing to pay bussers and servers' compensation for missed breaks, and manipulating bussers and servers' time records by deleting overtime hours in order to avoid paying their employees' due wages. (First Amended Complaint paragraphs 11-19.) Defendants' demurrer to the entire complaint, as it pertains to Defendant Kumar and Defendant Bist individually, is sustained in part and denied in part. The demurrer to the entire complaint, as it pertains to Defendant Kumar and Defendant Bist individually, is sustained as to all causes of action, except the twelfth cause of action. Individuals cannot be held liable for failure to pay wages merely on the basis of their status as corporate officers. (Reynolds v. Bement (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1075, 1086-1087.) Section 558 of the Labor Code does, however, allow for such individuals to be held personally liable if they "cause" Labor Code violations to occur. (Helm v. Alderwoods Grp. Inc. (2009) 696 F.Supp.2d 1057, 1074.) Here, Plaintiffs have alleged facts that Defendants Kumar and Bist caused the labor violation to occur. (First Amended Complaint paragraphs 4.) Thus, Defendants' demurrer to the twelfth cause of action, as it pertains to Defendant Kumar and Defendant Bist individually, is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H.
